summary: Package hijacks global console to exfiltrate log output to attacker-controlled Telegram and Firebase
details: On require of this package, index.js immediately invokes an IIFE that loads 66o.js, which replaces the global `console` object with a Proxy. Every subsequent `console.log`, `console.error`, and `console.dir` call in the installing process is stringified and POSTed to https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage with hardcoded chat IDs, and additional payloads are written via PUT to https://iiilll.firebaseio.com/ and https://i----i.firebaseio.com/. Application logs in typical Node services routinely contain error stacks, request bodies, tokens, and other sensitive data, so this constitutes a silent one-way exfiltration channel out of the installer's host. index.js additionally installs `globalThis.E`, whose `put()` method performs arbitrary PUTs to the same Firebase endpoint. Evidence: 66o.js:1 `console = new Proxy(console, {...})`; 66o.js:31 fetch to api.telegram.org/bot989543891:.../sendMessage; index.js:1-2 auto-running IIFE requiring './66o'; index.js:4-8 and index.js:96 globally exposed `E.put` to firebaseio.com. The hardcoded credentials in source are secondary — the primary harm is to anyone who installs and imports this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
